Computes exp(x), function result is nearly rounded. It will be correctly rounded to the theoretical value for 99.9% of input values, otherwise it will have a 1 ULP error.

paramTags.comment: Base of the logarithm, must be greater than 0.

returnTag.comment: Returns NaN if either argument is negative. If base is 0 and x is positive, 0 is returned. If base is positive and x is 0, Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY is returned. If both arguments are 0, the result is NaN.
returnTag.condition: methodResultID == (args[0] <= 0 || args[1] <= 0) ? Double.isNaN(methodResultID) : ((args[0] == 0 && args[1] > 0) ? 0 : ((args[0] > 0 && args[1] == 0) ? Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY : (args[0] == 0 && args[1] == 0) ? Double.isNaN(methodResultID) : methodResultID))
